data.title: Indian rupee to take cues from regional peers, bond traders eye liquidity moves
data.description:
Persistent foreign portfolio outflows may keep the Indian rupee on the defensive this week with the currency taking cues from regional peers, while government bonds will react to liquidity infusions by the central bank.

The rupee , rose slightly week-on-week to settle at 86.7125 against the U.S. dollar on Friday.

While the local unit's near-dated realized volatility eased last week, persistent selling of domestic stocks by overseas investors has kept it under pressure, which traders reckon is likely to persist.

Foreign investors have net sold over $11 billion of local equities over 2025 so far, contributing to the rupee being one of Asia's worst-performing currencies.

Strong interventions by the Reserve Bank of India have reduced speculative positioning against the currency, but the trend continues to be one of steady depreciation, said Abhilash Koikkara, head of forex and rates at Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

He expects the rupee to be in the 86.40 to 87.10 range in the near-term and weaken towards 88 over six months.

Meanwhile, focus will be on the Reserve Bank of India's $10 billion 3-year dollar-rupee buy/sell swap on February 28.

The swap follows a shorter-tenor $5 billion buy/sell swap conducted by the central bank last month as part of its measures to infuse liquidity in the banking system.

U.S. personal consumption expenditure inflation data due on Friday will also be in focus to gauge the future path of the Federal Reserve's policy rates.

Meanwhile, India's benchmark 10-year bond yield (IN067934G=CC), which ended marginally higher at 6.7065% on Friday, should move in the 6.67% to 6.74% range this week, traders said.

Over the last five weeks, the RBI bought 1 trillion rupees ($11.54 billion) of bonds via open market operations and another 388 billion rupees through secondary market purchases.

It has also infused around 440 billion rupees through a dollar/rupee buy/sell swap and injected 1.83 trillion rupees via long-term repos.

The focus should be towards maintaining durable liquidity, which has seen recent drain due to the rundown of FX reserves, said Dipanwita Mazumdar, an economist with Bank of Baroda.

India's inflation is seen aligning with the target of 4%, which opens up space for monetary policy to address concerns on the growth front, members of the rate-setting committee said in the minutes of the latest meeting released on Friday.

The RBI cut interest rates by 25 basis points at the February meeting.

Foreign investors have turned sellers of government bonds over the last two weeks.

($1 = 86.6380 Indian rupees)

data.title: Asian Currencies Strengthen Amid Prospects of Fed Rate Cuts — Market Talk
data.description: Asian currencies strengthen against the U.S. dollar in early trade amid prospects of Fed rate cuts that would decrease the allure of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. U.S. economic data released last Friday were disappointing, NAB's Rodrigo Catril says in commentary. Also, "playing to the emerging theme of a loss of U.S. exceptionalism, other services PMIs around the globe showed improvements," the senior FX strategist says. The weak U.S. data led to overnight-index-swap market bringing forward a 25 bp rate cut by the Fed to July from September, Catril adds. USD/KRW falls 0.2% to 1,431.80; AUD/USD edges 0.1% higher to 0.6369. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: NZX Midday Sector Update: Finance Soars, Health Services Struggles
data.description:
Finance stocks gained the most on the New Zealand exchange, rising past 1% at midday Monday.

Westpac Banking rose the highest in the sector, gaining about 1% in recent trade.

On the other hand, health services shares dropped past 3%.

Summerset Group (NZE:SNZ) shares dropped past 6% in recent trade to reach its lowest since October 2024.

data.title: Australia's Reece set for worst day ever after soft first-half result
data.description:
** Shares of plumbing products supplier Reece fall 14% to A$18.87, set for their worst day ever, if losses hold

** Co posts 19% drop in HY NPAT to A$181 million ($115.22 million), lower than consensus estimate of A$189 million

** UBS analysts say result marginally below due to softer Australian result, and while USA sales were in line with brokerage's estimate, margin still missed

** Management's guidance suggests risk remains in 2HFY25 with macro still a meaningful headwind in both ANZ and USA...this likely weighs on share price given REH's multiple remains well above normal levels - UBS

** Stock among worst performers in ASX 200 benchmark index

** Stock down 15.8% YTD, including day's moves

($1 = 1.5708 Australian dollars)

data.title: EVT Posts Higher Fiscal H1 Earnings, Lower Revenue; Shares Hit Two-Year High
data.description:
EVT reported Monday fiscal first-half earnings of AU$0.19 per diluted share, up from AU$0.167 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected AU$0.10 in diluted earnings per share.

Revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$649.1 million, compared with AU$658.9 million a year earlier.

The board declared an interim dividend of AU$0.16 per share, up from AU$0.14 in the previously reported period, payable March 20 to shareholders on record as of March 6.

The company's shares surged almost 12% in recent Monday trade, earlier hitting their highest since February 2023.

data.title: Australia's Iress slumps on lower-than-expected profit, forecast
data.description:
** Shares of finance-focussed software provider Iress fall as much as 18.3% to A$7.340, their lowest level since December 1, 2023

** Stock among the worst performers on benchmark index , which is little changed

** Co posts FY24 net profit after tax attributable (NPATA) of A$30.1 million ($19.18 million), missing consensus of A$43 million, according to Jefferies

** Co sees FY25 NPATA in a range of A$54 million to A$62 million, below Jefferies' consensus of A$70.4 million

** Stock set for sharpest one-day pct drop since August 2023

** 1 million shares change hands, 2.1x times the 30-day average

** Stock down 20.4% YTD, including session's moves

($1 = 1.5691 Australian dollars)

data.title: APA Group Plans AU$75 Million Gas Grid Expansion, Shares Rise 7%
data.description:
APA Group said that it will expand its east coast gas grid under a five-year plan, having secured commitments for an initial investment of around AU$75 million over the next two years, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

It expects the expansion to deliver an increase of around 24% in north-to-south gas transport capacity and new southern markets storage.

Around AU$40 million of the commitments will be used to deliver two enhancements to the the grid that have reached the final investment decision stage.

The remaining around AU$35 million will fund early works on the third, fourth, and fifth stages of the plan, to support the development of new grid expansion and storage projects over the medium term. These works will be conditional on early customer support, board approval, and any required regulatory approvals.

It will invest around AU$25 million to deliver the Moomba-to-Sydney ethane pipeline conversion project, which is targeting completion within the year. The project is planned to provide around an additional 20 terajoules per day from Moomba to Victoria or around 25 terajoules per day to Sydney.

It will also invest around AU$15 million for the MSP off-peak capacity expansion project to increase capacity in the summer months.

The firm's shares rose 7% in early trading on Monday.
